evals.prompt: Assess this idea: an Airtasker-style marketplace for tradespeople outside London.
evals.expected_output: A cold scorecard rating the idea High-friction on the marketplace axes, with an AVOID or HARD verdict and a constructive what-would-have-to-be-true.
expectations: All 9 rubric axes appear in the scorecard, each with a High-friction or Low-friction rating.
Sides, Liquidity geography, and Moat type are each rated High-friction.
The verdict label is AVOID or HARD and is accompanied by an X/9 axis tally.
The single biggest reason names the two-sided, local-liquidity, or marketplace dynamic.
A 'what would have to be true to make it work' line is present.

evals.prompt: Score this idea: a vertical project-management tool ('Linear for X') for a knowledge-work niche I already understand well.
evals.expected_output: A cold scorecard rating the structural axes Low-friction and the founder-edge axis Low-friction, with a VIABLE or STRONG verdict and all three gates addressed.
expectations: All 9 rubric axes appear in the scorecard, each with a friction rating.
Sides and Liquidity geography are each rated Low-friction.
Founder edge transfer (axis 8) is rated Low-friction.
Gate B (AI-resilience) and Gate C (cost of entry) are each explicitly evaluated with a Pass or Fail.
The verdict label is VIABLE or STRONG.

evals.prompt: Assess this idea: a subscription compliance-certificate tool for MCS-registered heat-pump installers.
evals.expected_output: A full assessment produced by a spawned cold sub-agent that judged the idea on its own terms, with all axes and all three gates scored.
expectations: A sub-agent is spawned via the Agent tool to perform the scoring rather than scoring inline.
The scoring sub-agent's prompt contains only the idea statement and the rubric, with no content from the calling conversation.
The final output contains no detail that was only available in the surrounding conversation.
All 9 axes and all three gates (willingness-to-pay, AI-resilience, cost-of-entry) are scored, and a verdict label with an X/9 tally is present.

evals.prompt: Anyway, I'm still half-thinking about that trades thing, but let's move on.
evals.expected_output: Silence. The skill recognises a passing mention with no request for a verdict and does not fire.
expectations: The skill does not fire and no scorecard is produced.
No verdict label is presented; the skill stays silent and lets the conversation continue.
